question: How many distinct institutions are recorded?
How many projects have 'cancer' in their title?
How many agencies have an abbreviation starting with 'N'?
For each study section, how many projects were reviewed by it that started in the fiscal year '2022'?
gold_sparql:
SELECT ?study_section_name (COUNT(DISTINCT ?project) AS ?project_count) FROM <https://omnix.dev/graphs/demo-tenant/kg/holdout-v2-nih-reporter-non-clinical> WHERE {
  ?study_section <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#type> <https://omnix.dev/holdout-v2/holdout-v2-nih-reporter-non-clinical/type/StudySection> .
  ?study_section <https://omnix.dev/holdout-v2/holdout-v2-nih-reporter-non-clinical/pred/study_section_name> ?study_section_name .
  ?project <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#type> <https://omnix.dev/holdout-v2/holdout-v2-nih-reporter-non-clinical/type/Project> .
  ?project <https://omnix.dev/holdout-v2/holdout-v2-nih-reporter-non-clinical/pred/reviewed_by> ?study_section .
  ?project <https://omnix.dev/holdout-v2/holdout-v2-nih-reporter-non-clinical/pred/fiscal_year> "2022" .
} GROUP BY ?study_section_name
ORDER BY DESC(?project_count)

import pandas as pd

# Merge Project and StudySection on study_section_id
merged_df = pd.merge(dfs['Project'], dfs['StudySection'], on='study_section_id')

# Filter for projects that started in fiscal year 2022
filtered_df = merged_df[merged_df['fiscal_year'] == 2022]

# Group by study_section_name and count the number of projects
counts = filtered_df.groupby('study_section_name').size().reset_index(name='project_count')

# Convert to a list of [study_section_name, project_count]
result = counts.values.tolist()

per_seed.code:
import pandas as pd

# Merge Project with Institution to filter for 'UNITED STATES'
# Merge with StudySection to ensure the project was reviewed by a study section
# Merge with Investigator to get the names

merged_df = dfs['Project'].merge(dfs['Institution'], on='institution_id')
merged_df = merged_df[merged_df['org_country'] == 'UNITED STATES']

# Ensure study_section_id is not null and exists in StudySection table
merged_df = merged_df.merge(dfs['StudySection'], on='study_section_id')

# Get investigator names
final_df = merged_df.merge(dfs['Investigator'], on='investigator_id')

result = final_df['investigator_name'].unique().tolist()

per_seed.code:
import pandas as pd

# Merge Project with Institution to filter by country
project_inst = pd.merge(dfs['Project'], dfs['Institution'], on='institution_id')

# Filter for 'UNITED STATES'
us_projects = project_inst[project_inst['org_country'] == 'UNITED STATES']

# Ensure study_section_id is not null (reviewed by a study section)
reviewed_us_projects = us_projects[us_projects['study_section_id'].notnull()]

# Merge with Investigator to get names
final_df = pd.merge(reviewed_us_projects, dfs['Investigator'], on='investigator_id')

# Get unique investigator names
result = final_df['investigator_name'].unique().tolist()

per_seed.code:
import pandas as pd

# Merge Project with Institution to filter for 'UNITED STATES'
# Merge with StudySection to ensure the project was reviewed by a study section
# Merge with Investigator to get the names

merged_df = dfs['Project'].merge(dfs['Institution'], on='institution_id')
merged_df = merged_df[merged_df['org_country'] == 'UNITED STATES']

# Ensure study_section_id is not null and exists in StudySection table
merged_df = merged_df.merge(dfs['StudySection'], on='study_section_id')

# Get investigator names
final_df = merged_df.merge(dfs['Investigator'], on='investigator_id')

result = final_df['investigator_name'].unique().tolist()
